title: Self-host — run fab install on this repo + verify doctor + commit generated bootstrap files
description: Dogfood the new pipeline on this repo. After TASK-01..06 land: build packages (pnpm -r build), run `fab install` from the local dist, verify doctor reports status='ok', and commit the generated files. Provides golden state for CI doctor runs and demonstrates end-to-end correctness of the rc.19 pipeline.

files.change: Created by `fab install` from BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL. Verify byte-equal to packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL export.

Existing CLAUDE.md (if any user content) preserved; idempotent line-add inserts `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` (project-rules.md doesn't exist in this repo so no second @-import). Verify final file contains the @-import line exactly once.

Created by writeCursorBootstrapManagedBlock with `---
alwaysApply: true
description: Fabric Protocol bootstrap rules
---

` front-matter + managed block containing buildManagedBlockBody. mkdirp `.cursor/rules/` directory if needed.

Existing repo-root AGENTS.md (L1-L8 user content + L10-L21 legacy fabric:knowledge-base managed block) — needs migration. Step 1: run `fab doctor --fix` to migrate marker fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap. Step 2: install rewrites managed block body to byte-equal canonical content. Verify: L1-L8 user content preserved; managed block uses new marker; block body byte-equals .fabric/AGENTS.md (project-rules.md absent).

implementation: From repo root, run `node packages/cli/dist/bin/fab.js install --target $PWD` (or local-dev equivalent)
Verify `.fabric/AGENTS.md` created with BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL content (sha256 or diff against the shared export)
Run `fab doctor --fix` to migrate the existing fabric:knowledge-base marker in root AGENTS.md (per TASK-04 logic). Verify events.jsonl contains bootstrap_marker_migrated event
Re-run `fab doctor` (no --fix): expect status='ok' across all checks (L1 + L2 + marker_migration all pass)
Verify CLAUDE.md @-import line idempotent: cat CLAUDE.md | grep -c '@.fabric/AGENTS.md' === 1
Verify .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc has alwaysApply: true and managed block
Verify root AGENTS.md managed block body byte-equals .fabric/AGENTS.md content (no project-rules.md in this repo)
git add the 4 affected files + .fabric/events.jsonl (migration event)
Stage the changes for the rc.19 commit (do NOT commit in this task — left to PR author)

reference.pattern: Dogfooding pattern from rc.16 self-host of banner-i18n — install on the repo itself to validate end-to-end pipeline
reference.examples: rc.16 commit eb7a2e7 (banner-i18n self-host) — install pipeline runs end-to-end on this repo and produces golden state for CI

criteria: .fabric/AGENTS.md exists at repo root with content byte-equal to BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL (verified via diff or sha256)
.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc exists with `alwaysApply: true` in front-matter AND exactly one fabric:bootstrap marker pair
Root AGENTS.md L1-L8 user content preserved byte-identical to pre-install state
Root AGENTS.md managed block (between fabric:bootstrap:begin/end) body byte-equals .fabric/AGENTS.md content
Root AGENTS.md contains ZERO fabric:knowledge-base substrings (migration succeeded)
`fab doctor` (no --fix) reports status='ok' on all rc.19-relevant checks (bootstrap_anchor_missing, bootstrap_marker_migration_required, bootstrap_snapshot_drift, managed_block_drift)
Re-running `fab install` produces zero file modifications (idempotent)

rationale.chosen_approach: Run the full install + doctor cycle on this repo to validate end-to-end correctness. Captures any bugs that pure unit/integration tests miss (e.g. real filesystem interactions, real existing CLAUDE.md content).
alternatives_considered: Skip self-host until release candidate stabilizes (delays end-to-end validation)
Run on a separate test repo (loses the migration-from-legacy benefit that this repo's existing fabric:knowledge-base markers provide)
decision_factors: Tests TASK-04 migration on real legacy state (this repo's AGENTS.md L10-L21)
rationale.tradeoffs: Repo changes in the rc.19 commit. Acceptable — this is intentional dogfood.

manual_checks: head -4 .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc → expect YAML front-matter with alwaysApply: true
Run `fab doctor`, capture stdout, verify zero fixable_errors
success_metrics: End-to-end install → doctor cycle completes with zero errors
Migration from this repo's legacy marker state succeeds

risks.description: Existing root AGENTS.md L1-L8 user content may have edge cases (UTF-8 BOM, trailing whitespace) that confuse the managed-block writer
risks.mitigation: Inspect AGENTS.md byte content pre-install (hexdump -C | head); if BOM present, install writer must preserve it as pre-block user content

risks.description: .cursor/rules/ may already exist as a single file (rc.18 legacy state); deletion needs to happen before mkdirp
risks.mitigation: TASK-03 writeCursorBootstrapManagedBlock explicitly handles this case (delete legacy single-file `.cursor/rules` before creating directory). Verify the code path executes correctly during self-host.
